task: Write a sales page for a $99/month project management SaaS targeting freelancers.
expect: Uses a recognisable framework (AIDA, PAS, or BAB) to structure the page flow
Includes a compelling headline, social proof section, at least one CTA, and objection-handling section
Speaks to freelancer-specific pain points (juggling clients, invoicing, time tracking) rather than generic project management benefits

task: Create a 5-email welcome sequence for a B2B SaaS trial that converts to paid.
expect: Each email has a clear singular purpose and builds on the previous (e.g. welcome, quick win, deeper feature, social proof, conversion)
Includes specific subject lines for each email and defines send timing/triggers
Creates urgency without being pushy — uses trial expiry, value realisation, or peer benchmarks rather than artificial scarcity

task: Our landing page converts at 0.5%. Here's the current copy. Diagnose what's wrong and rewrite the hero section.
expect: Identifies specific copy problems (weak value prop, feature-focused instead of benefit-focused, unclear CTA, missing urgency)
Rewrites the hero section with a stronger value proposition tied to a measurable outcome
Suggests an A/B test variant or testing approach to validate the new copy against the original
